title: Xerophysa physodes
taxonomy: Xerophysa physodes is a species of plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is native to tropical Asia. Xerophysa physodes is commonly known as the Asian pigweed or Asian prostrate pigweed. The plant is an annual or perennial herb with a prostrate or ascending growth habit. It is often found in wet or moist areas, such as riversides, lakeshores, and wet meadows.

intro: Xerophysa physodes, also known as Guduchi or Amrita, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly used in Ayurvedic medicine. The health benefits of Xerophysa physodes include its immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, inflammation, and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
